event_overview.trigger: A canonical record is modified via the update operation (PSD-0001). In Phase 1, editable fields are limited since the 5 hierarchy columns are locked; this event is defined for forward compatibility as additional metadata fields are added in future phases.
event_overview.business_description: Signals to downstream systems that a canonical instrument record has been modified. Consumers should call the Canonical Query API (PSD-0002 FLOW-A) using the canonical_key to retrieve the latest record state. Distinct from canonical.status.changed — that event is raised specifically for status lifecycle changes.

registered_consumers.purpose: Detect canonical record changes to refresh source-to-canonical mappings
Detect canonical record changes to refresh enrichment caches

fields.description: The canonical key hash of the updated record. Consumers use this to call the Canonical Query API for the latest record state.

mappings.consumer_action: Call Canonical Query API (PSD-0002 FLOW-A) to fetch updated record and refresh source-to-canonical mappings
Call Canonical Query API (PSD-0002 FLOW-A) to fetch updated record and invalidate enrichment cache entry

schema_evolution.compatibility_strategy: Backward compatible — new optional fields may be added without a version bump; no existing fields may be removed or have their type changed without a major version increment
schema_evolution.breaking_changes: Topic rename, field removal, field type change, partition key change — all require major version bump and 30-day consumer migration notice
schema_evolution.deprecation_policy: Deprecated fields must be flagged in the schema with a deprecation notice and remain present for a minimum of 30 days before removal
schema_evolution.versioning: Breaking changes result in a new event type (e.g., canonical.record.updated.v2) published in parallel until all consumers have migrated

delivery_sla.guarantee: At-least-once — consumers must be idempotent on canonical_key
delivery_sla.ordering: Ordered per canonical_key within a partition — no global ordering guarantee
delivery_sla.latency: TBD — within platform event pipeline SLA
delivery_sla.retry_policy: Standard Kafka producer retry — platform standard
delivery_sla.dead_letter_topic: nmd.canonical.record.updated.dlt — failed consumer messages routed here after max retries
delivery_sla.idempotency: Consumers must deduplicate on canonical_key; duplicate events may be produced in failure/retry scenarios

security.authentication: Kafka ACL — mTLS or SASL (TBD — platform standard)

authorization.consume: Data Ingestion Module (FRD-NMD-0002), Query & Export Module (FRD-NMD-0003)

security.pii_handling: No PII in payload — canonical_key is a deterministic hash; no personal or sensitive data transmitted
security.compliance: Internal classification — no regulatory data handling requirements for this event

alerting: Alert on consumer lag exceeding threshold (TBD)
observability.tracing: Propagate trace ID in CloudEvents extension header for end-to-end tracing
observability.logging: Producer logs canonical_key and event ID on each publish; consumer logs canonical_key on receipt

entries.author: Leo
entries.description: Initial draft — lightweight notification event carrying canonical_key only; defined for forward compatibility as canonical record gains additional editable fields in future phases
